heading: Other requirements applicable to certain private activity bonds
label: 147 Other requirements applicable to certain private activity bonds

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: Except as provided in subsection (h), a private activity bond shall not be a qualified bond for any period during which it is held by a person who is a substantial user of the facilities or by a related person of such a substantial user.

children.label: A
children.text: 2 or more persons if the relationship between such persons would result in a disallowance of losses under section 267 or 707(b),

children.label: B
children.text: 2 or more persons which are members of the same controlled group of corporations (as defined in section 1563(a), except that “more than 50 percent” shall be substituted for “at least 80 percent” each place it appears therein),

children.label: C
children.text: a partnership and each of its partners (and their spouses and minor children), and

children.label: D
children.text: an S corporation and each of its shareholders (and their spouses and minor children).

children.heading: Maturity may not exceed 120 percent of economic life
children.label: b Maturity may not exceed 120 percent of economic life
A
children.text: the average maturity of the bonds issued as part of such issue, exceeds

children.label: B
children.text: 120 percent of the average reasonably expected economic life of the facilities being financed with the net proceeds of such issue.

children.label: A
children.text: the average maturity of any issue shall be determined by taking into account the respective issue prices of the bonds issued as part of such issue, and

children.label: B
children.text: the average reasonably expected economic life of the facilities being financed with any issue shall be determined by taking into account the respective cost of such facilities.

children.label: i
children.text: the date on which the bonds are issued, or

children.label: ii
children.text: the date on which the facility is placed in service (or expected to be placed in service).

children.heading: Land not taken into account
children.label: i Land not taken into account
children.text: Except as provided in clause (ii), land shall not be taken into account under paragraph (1)(B).

children.heading: Issues where 25 percent or more of proceeds used to finance land
children.label: ii Issues where 25 percent or more of proceeds used to finance land
children.text: If 25 percent or more of the net proceeds of any issue is to be used to finance land, such land shall be taken into account under paragraph (1)(B) and shall be treated as having an economic life of 30 years.

children.heading: Special rule for pooled financing of 501(c)(3) organization
children.label: 4 Special rule for pooled financing of 501(c)(3) organization

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: At the election of the issuer, a qualified 501(c)(3) bond shall be treated as meeting the requirements of paragraph (1) if such bond meets the requirements of subparagraph (B).

children.label: i
children.text: 95 percent or more of the net proceeds of the issue of which such bond is a part are to be used to make or finance loans to 2 or more 501(c)(3) organizations or governmental units for acquisition of property to be used by such organizations,

children.label: ii
children.text: each loan described in clause (i) satisfies the requirements of paragraph (1) (determined by treating each loan as a separate issue),

children.label: iii
children.text: before such bond is issued, a demand survey was conducted which shows a demand for financing greater than an amount equal to 120 percent of the lendable proceeds of such issue, and

children.label: iv
children.text: 95 percent or more of the net proceeds of such issue are to be loaned to 501(c)(3) organizations or governmental units within 1 year of issuance and, to the extent there are any unspent proceeds after such 1-year period, bonds issued as part of such issue are to be redeemed as soon as possible thereafter (and in no event later than 18 months after issuance).

children.heading: Special rule for certain FHA insured loans
children.label: 5 Special rule for certain FHA insured loans
children.text: Paragraph (1) shall not apply to any bond issued as part of an issue 95 percent or more of the net proceeds of which are to be used to finance mortgage loans insured under FHA 242 or under a similar Federal Housing Administration program (as in effect on the date of the enactment of the Tax Reform Act of 1986) where the loan term approved by such Administration plus the maximum maturity of debentures which could be issued by such Administration in satisfaction of its obligations exceeds the term permitted under paragraph (1).

children.heading: Limitation on use for land acquisition
children.label: c Limitation on use for land acquisition
A
children.text: it is issued as part of an issue and 25 percent or more of the net proceeds of such issue are to be used (directly or indirectly) for the acquisition of land (or an interest therein), or

children.label: B
children.text: any portion of the proceeds of such issue is to be used (directly or indirectly) for the acquisition of land (or an interest therein) to be used for farming purposes.

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: If the requirements of subparagraph (B) are met with respect to any land, paragraph (1) shall not apply to such land, and subsection (d) shall not apply to property to be used thereon for farming purposes, but only to the extent of expenditures (financed with the proceeds of the issue) not in excess of $450,000.

children.label: i
children.text: such land is to be used for farming purposes, and

children.label: ii
children.text: such land is to be acquired by an individual who is a first-time farmer, who will be the principal user of such land, and who will materially and substantially participate on the farm of which such land is a part in the operation of such farm.

children.label: I
children.text: has not at any time had any direct or indirect ownership interest in substantial farmland in the operation of which such individual materially participated, and

children.label: II
children.text: has not received financing under this paragraph in an amount which, when added to the financing to be provided under this paragraph, exceeds the amount in effect under subparagraph (A).

children.heading: Aggregation rules
children.label: ii Aggregation rules
children.text: Any ownership or material participation, or financing received, by an individual’s spouse or minor child shall be treated as ownership and material participation, or financing received, by the individual.

children.heading: Insolvent farmer
children.label: iii Insolvent farmer
children.text: For purposes of clause (i), farmland which was previously owned by the individual and was disposed of while such individual was insolvent shall be disregarded if section 108 applied to indebtedness with respect to such farmland.

children.heading: Farm
children.label: D Farm
children.text: For purposes of this paragraph, the term “farm” has the meaning given such term by section 6420(c)(2).

children.heading: Substantial farmland
children.label: E Substantial farmland
children.text: For purposes of this paragraph, the term “substantial farmland” means any parcel of land unless such parcel is smaller than 30 percent of the median size of a farm in the county in which such parcel is located.

children.label: i
children.text: of a character subject to the allowance for depreciation,

children.label: ii
children.text: the original use of which does not begin with such farmer, and

children.label: iii
children.text: which is to be used for farming purposes,

children.label: i
children.text: the acquisition price is for the fair market value of such land or property, and

children.label: ii
children.text: subsequent to such acquisition, the related person does not have a financial interest in the farming operation with respect to which the bond proceeds are to be used.

children.label: i
children.text: such dollar amount, multiplied by

children.label: ii
children.text: the cost-of-living adjustment determined under section 1(f)(3) for the calendar year, determined by substituting “calendar year 2007” for “calendar year 2016” in subparagraph (A)(ii) thereof.

children.heading: Exception for certain land acquired for environmental purposes, etc.
children.label: 3 Exception for certain land acquired for environmental purposes, etc.
A
children.text: such land is acquired for noise abatement or wetland preservation, or for future use as an airport, mass commuting facility, high-speed intercity rail facility, dock, or wharf, and

children.label: B
children.text: there is not other significant use of such land.

children.heading: Acquisition of existing property not permitted
children.label: d Acquisition of existing property not permitted

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: Except as provided in subsection (h), a private activity bond shall not be a qualified bond if issued as part of an issue and any portion of the net proceeds of such issue is to be used for the acquisition of any property (or an interest therein) unless the 1st use of such property is pursuant to such acquisition.

children.label: A
children.text: the rehabilitation expenditures with respect to such building, equal or exceed

children.label: B
children.text: 15 percent of the portion of the cost of acquiring such building (and equipment) financed with the net proceeds of the issue.

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: Except as provided in this paragraph, the term “rehabilitation expenditures” means any amount properly chargeable to capital account which is incurred by the person acquiring the building for property (or additions or improvements to property) in connection with the rehabilitation of a building. In the case of an integrated operation contained in a building before its acquisition, such term includes rehabilitating existing equipment in such building or replacing it with equipment having substantially the same function. For purposes of this subparagraph, any amount incurred by a successor to the person acquiring the building or by the seller under a sales contract with such person shall be treated as incurred by such person.

children.heading: Certain expenditures not included
children.label: B Certain expenditures not included
children.text: The term “rehabilitation expenditures” does not include any expenditure described in section 47(c)(2)(B).

children.heading: Period during which expenditures must be incurred
children.label: C Period during which expenditures must be incurred
i
children.text: the date on which the building was acquired, or

children.label: ii
children.text: the date on which the bond was issued.

children.heading: Special rule for certain projects
children.label: 4 Special rule for certain projects
children.text: In the case of a project involving 2 or more buildings, this subsection shall be applied on a project basis.

children.heading: No portion of bonds may be issued for skyboxes, airplanes, gambling establishments, etc.
children.label: e No portion of bonds may be issued for skyboxes, airplanes, gambling establishments, etc.
children.text: A private activity bond shall not be a qualified bond if issued as part of an issue and any portion of the proceeds of such issue is to be used to provide any airplane, skybox or other private luxury box, health club facility, facility primarily used for gambling, or store the principal business of which is the sale of alcoholic beverages for consumption off premises. The preceding sentence shall not apply to any fixed-wing aircraft equipped for, and exclusively dedicated to providing, acute care emergency medical services (within the meaning of section 4261(g)(2)).

children.heading: Public approval required for private activity bonds
children.label: f Public approval required for private activity bonds

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: A private activity bond shall not be a qualified bond unless such bond satisfies the requirements of paragraph (2).

children.label: I
children.text: which issued such bond, or

children.label: II
children.text: on behalf of which such bond was issued, and

children.label: ii
children.text: each governmental unit having jurisdiction over the area in which any facility, with respect to which financing is to be provided from the net proceeds of such issue, is located (except that if more than 1 governmental unit within a State has jurisdiction over the entire area within such State in which such facility is located, only 1 such unit need approve such issue).

children.heading: Approval by a governmental unit
children.label: B Approval by a governmental unit
i
children.text: by the applicable elected representative of such governmental unit after a public hearing following reasonable public notice, or

children.label: ii
children.text: by voter referendum of such governmental unit.

children.heading: Special rules for approval of facility
children.label: C Special rules for approval of facility
i
children.text: which is issued pursuant to such plan within 3 years after the date of the 1st issue pursuant to the approval, and

children.label: ii
children.text: all or substantially all of the proceeds of which are to be used to finance such facility or to refund previous financing under such plan.

children.heading: Refunding bonds
children.label: D Refunding bonds
children.text: No approval under subparagraph (A) shall be necessary with respect to any bond which is issued to refund (other than to advance refund) a bond approved under subparagraph (A) (or treated as approved under subparagraph (C)) unless the average maturity date of the issue of which the refunding bond is a part is later than the average maturity date of the bonds to be refunded by such issue. For purposes of the preceding sentence, average maturity shall be determined in accordance with subsection (b)(2)(A).

children.label: I
children.text: an elected legislative body of such unit, or

children.label: II
children.text: the chief elected executive officer, the chief elected State legal officer of the executive branch, or any other elected official of such unit designated for purposes of this paragraph by such chief elected executive officer or by State law.

children.label: I
children.text: which is the next higher governmental unit with such a representative, and

children.label: II
children.text: from which the authority of the governmental unit with no such representative is derived.

children.heading: Special rule for approval of airports or high-speed intercity rail facilities
children.label: 3 Special rule for approval of airports or high-speed intercity rail facilities
A
children.text: the proceeds of an issue are to be used to finance a facility or facilities located at an airport or high-speed intercity rail facilities, and

children.label: B
children.text: the governmental unit issuing such bonds is the owner or operator of such airport or high-speed intercity rail facilities,

children.heading: Special rules for scholarship funding bond issues and volunteer fire department bond issues
children.label: 4 Special rules for scholarship funding bond issues and volunteer fire department bond issues

children.heading: Scholarship funding bonds
children.label: A Scholarship funding bonds
children.text: In the case of a qualified scholarship funding bond, any governmental unit which made a request described in section 150(d)(2)(B) with respect to the issuer of such bond shall be treated for purposes of paragraph (2) of this subsection as the governmental unit on behalf of which such bond was issued. Where more than one governmental unit within a State has made a request described in section 150(d)(2)(B), the State may also be treated for purposes of paragraph (2) of this subsection as the governmental unit on behalf of which such bond was issued.

children.heading: Volunteer fire department bonds
children.label: B Volunteer fire department bonds
children.text: In the case of a bond of a volunteer fire department which meets the requirements of section 150(e), the political subdivision described in section 150(e)(2)(B) with respect to such department shall be treated for purposes of paragraph (2) of this subsection as the governmental unit on behalf of which such bond was issued.

children.heading: Restriction on issuance costs financed by issue
children.label: g Restriction on issuance costs financed by issue

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: A private activity bond shall not be a qualified bond if the issuance costs financed by the issue (of which such bond is a part) exceed 2 percent of the proceeds of the issue.

children.heading: Special rule for small mortgage revenue bond issues
children.label: 2 Special rule for small mortgage revenue bond issues
children.text: In the case of an issue of qualified mortgage bonds or qualified veterans’ mortgage bonds, paragraph (1) shall be applied by substituting “3.5 percent” for “2 percent” if the proceeds of the issue do not exceed $20,000,000.

children.heading: Certain rules not to apply to certain bonds
children.label: h Certain rules not to apply to certain bonds

children.heading: Mortgage revenue bonds and qualified student loan bonds
children.label: 1 Mortgage revenue bonds and qualified student loan bonds
children.text: Subsections (a), (b), (c), and (d) shall not apply to any qualified mortgage bond, qualified veterans’ mortgage bond, or qualified student loan bond.

children.heading: Qualified 501(c)(3) bonds
children.label: 2 Qualified 501(c)(3) bonds
children.text: Subsections (a), (c), and (d) shall not apply to any qualified 501(c)(3) bond and subsection (e) shall be applied as if it did not contain “health club facility” with respect to such a bond.

children.heading: Exempt facility bonds for qualified public-private schools
children.label: 3 Exempt facility bonds for qualified public-private schools
children.text: Subsection (c) shall not apply to any exempt facility bond issued as part of an issue described in section 142(a)(13) (relating to qualified public educational facilities).
